2606.22442.title: Efficient Multimodal Clinical Question Answering for Pulmonary Embolism Risk Assessment
2606.22442.paper_summary: Pulmonary embolism (PE) is a high risk cardiopulmonary condition whose management requires both timely diagnosis and reliable assessment of future clinical risk. Because PE care routinely combines computed tomography pulmonary angiography (CTPA), radiology interpretation, and longitudinal electronic health record (EHR) evidence, it provides a clinically meaningful setting for evaluating compact multimodal language models. In this work, we build a benchmark using efficient multimodal large language models (MLLMs) on INSPECT, a multimodal PE dataset containing 23,248 CTPA studies from 19,402 patients. We formulate eight diagnostic and prognostic tasks as structured clinical question answering problems and evaluate on typical efficient MLLMs under CTPA-Only, EHR-Only, and CTPA+EHR settings with zero-shot and few-shot prompting. Results show that Gemma4 E4B and Gemma4 E2B perform more strongly when EHR evidence is available, especially under CTPA+EHR input. Task level analysis further shows that PE diagnosis achieves higher performance than prognostic tasks, particularly readmission prediction. These observations suggest that compact multimodal models have the great potential in early stage PE risk detection and explanation.
2606.22442.paper_summary_zh: 肺栓塞（PE）是一種高風險的心肺疾病，其管理需要及時診斷和可靠的未來臨床風險評估。<lb/>由於PE護理通常結合了計算機斷層肺動脈造影（CTPA）、放射學解讀和長期電子健康記錄（EHR）證據，因此它為評估緊湊的多模態語言模型提供了一個臨床上有意義的環境。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們利用高效的多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）建立了一個基準，該基準基於INSPECT，一個包含19,402名患者的23,248個CTPA研究的多模態PE數據集。<lb/>我們將八個診斷和預後任務構建為結構化的臨床問題回答問題，並在CTPA-Only、EHR-Only和CTPA+EHR設置下，針對典型的高效MLLM進行零樣本和少樣本提示的評估。<lb/>結果顯示，當EHR證據可用時，Gemma4 E4B和Gemma4 E2B的表現更強，特別是在CTPA+EHR輸入下。<lb/>任務層級分析進一步顯示，PE診斷的表現優於預後任務，特別是再入院預測。<lb/>這些觀察表明，緊湊的多模態模型在早期PE風險檢測和解釋方面具有巨大的潛力。
2606.22442.author: Xiangyuan Xue et.al.
2606.22442.authors: Xiangyuan Xue, Yang Yu, Yan Gao, Junyan Wang, Bin Chen, Lingyan Ruan, Ting Dang, Hong Jia
